steps.title: Use a two-point table
steps.content: The line has slope $-\frac{1}{4}$ and passes through $(0,19)$. Move 4 units right and 1 unit down to get another point, $(4,18)$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content: The slope is $m=-\frac{1}{4}$ and the $y$-intercept is $b=19$, so the equation is $y=-\frac{1}{4}x+19$. A choice with the right slope but the wrong intercept. The expression matches both. The other choices have the wrong slope sign. B is correct.
